title: Future return accidentally used as model feature
code:
df['future_return'] = df['close_usd'].pct_change().shift(-1)
features = ['future_return', 'volume_usd']
model.fit(df[features], df['signal'])

title: Next-day return feature leaks the prediction horizon
code:
df['next_return'] = df['close_usd'].pct_change().shift(-1)
X = df[['next_return', 'market_cap_usd']].dropna()
y = (df['close_usd'].pct_change() > 0).astype(int).loc[X.index]

title: Signal uses future volume change
code:
df['future_volume_change'] = df['volume_usd'].pct_change().shift(-1)
df['signal'] = (df['future_volume_change'] > 0).astype(int)
df['strategy_return'] = df['signal'] * df['close_usd'].pct_change()

title: Feature matrix includes the shifted target series
code:
df['future_ret'] = df['close_usd'].pct_change().shift(-1)
df['target'] = (df['future_ret'] > 0).astype(int)
features = ['market_cap_usd', 'volume_usd', 'future_ret']
classifier.fit(df[features].dropna(), df['target'].dropna())

title: Full-sample normalization before train/test split
code:
df['z_volume'] = (df['volume_usd'] - df['volume_usd'].mean()) / df['volume_usd'].std()
train = df[df['date'] < '2026-04-11']
test = df[df['date'] >= '2026-04-11']

title: StandardScaler fit_transform runs before chronological split
code:
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
scaled = StandardScaler().fit_transform(df[['close_usd', 'volume_usd']])
train = scaled[:6]
test = scaled[6:]

title: Min-max feature uses full sample extrema
code:
df['normalized_close'] = (df['close_usd'] - df['close_usd'].min()) / (df['close_usd'].max() - df['close_usd'].min())
train = df.iloc[:6]
test = df.iloc[6:]

title: Market cap z-score is computed across the full dataset
code:
df['z_market_cap'] = (df['market_cap_usd'] - df['market_cap_usd'].mean()) / df['market_cap_usd'].std()
cutoff = '2026-04-10'
train = df[df['date'] < cutoff]
test = df[df['date'] >= cutoff]

title: Random split used for financial time series
code:
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
train, test = train_test_split(df, test_size=0.25, shuffle=True, random_state=7)

title: Sorted market data is randomized after date ordering
code:
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
df = df.sort_values('date')
train, test = train_test_split(df, test_size=0.30, shuffle=True)

title: Return classifier uses shuffled train-test split
code:
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
df['ret_1d'] = df['close_usd'].pct_change()
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(df[['volume_usd']], df['ret_1d'] > 0, shuffle=True, random_state=42)

title: Backtest evaluation randomly splits time-indexed rows
code:
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
df = df.set_index('date')
train_rows, test_rows = train_test_split(df, test_size=0.2, shuffle=True)
score = evaluate_strategy(train_rows, test_rows)

title: Backtest reports gross returns without costs
code:
df['position'] = (df['close_usd'].pct_change() > 0).astype(int)
df['strategy_return'] = df['position'].shift(1) * df['close_usd'].pct_change()
sharpe = df['strategy_return'].mean() / df['strategy_return'].std()

title: Momentum strategy annualizes gross Sharpe without frictions
code:
df['signal'] = (df['close_usd'].pct_change().rolling(3).mean().shift(1) > 0).astype(int)
df['strategy_return'] = df['signal'] * df['close_usd'].pct_change()
sharpe = 252 ** 0.5 * df['strategy_return'].mean() / df['strategy_return'].std()

title: High-turnover volume strategy omits transaction costs
code:
df['signal'] = (df['volume_usd'] > df['volume_usd'].rolling(2).mean()).astype(int)
df['strategy_return'] = df['signal'].shift(1) * df['close_usd'].pct_change()
sharpe = df['strategy_return'].mean() / df['strategy_return'].std()

title: Transaction cost variable is declared but never applied
code:
transaction_cost_bps = 10
df['position'] = (df['close_usd'].pct_change().shift(1) > 0).astype(int)
df['strategy_return'] = df['position'] * df['close_usd'].pct_change()
sharpe = df['strategy_return'].mean() / df['strategy_return'].std()

title: Report claims state-of-the-art performance without baseline
code: The agentic strategy is clearly state-of-the-art and beats all alternatives. We omit baseline comparisons because the equity curve is visually compelling.

title: Report declares outperformance from visual inspection only
code: The chart clearly shows this BTC timing model outperforming buy-and-hold. No baseline table is included because the visual evidence is enough.

title: Small-sample writeup overstates generalization
code: This short BTC sample proves the strategy will beat all alternatives in live trading. We report no baseline and no robustness checks.

title: Simple shifted momentum with explicit costs
code:
df['ret_1d'] = df['close_usd'].pct_change()
df['signal'] = (df['ret_1d'].rolling(3).mean().shift(1) > 0).astype(int)
df['turnover'] = df['signal'].diff().abs().fillna(0)
df['strategy_return'] = df['signal'].shift(1) * df['ret_1d'] - 0.001 * df['turnover']

title: Scaler is fit on training window and applied to test window
code:
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
train = df[df['date'] < '2026-04-11'].copy()
test = df[df['date'] >= '2026-04-11'].copy()
scaler = StandardScaler().fit(train[['volume_usd']])
train['z_volume'] = scaler.transform(train[['volume_usd']])
test['z_volume'] = scaler.transform(test[['volume_usd']])

title: Chronological train-test split preserves time ordering
code:
df = df.sort_values('date')
train = df.iloc[:7]
test = df.iloc[7:]
model.fit(train[['volume_usd']], train['close_usd'].pct_change().fillna(0) > 0)

title: Report describes a descriptive baseline without exaggerated claims
code: This BTC sample is used as a descriptive benchmark only. The report does not claim statistical superiority and compares the strategy to buy-and-hold before discussing limitations.

title: Rebalance strategy subtracts turnover-based costs
code:
df['ret_1d'] = df['close_usd'].pct_change()
df['signal'] = (df['volume_usd'].shift(1) > df['volume_usd'].rolling(3).mean().shift(1)).astype(int)
df['turnover'] = df['signal'].diff().abs().fillna(0)
df['strategy_return'] = df['signal'].shift(1) * df['ret_1d'] - 0.0015 * df['turnover']
sharpe = df['strategy_return'].mean() / df['strategy_return'].std()
